briefdescription.para: ADC channel gain factors.
VDD.
VDD/2.
VDD/3.
VDD/4.
Internal.
External, input 0.
External, input 1.
ADC references.
The sequence should be continued normally.
The sampling should be repeated.

detaileddescription.para: New samples or sample should be read from the ADC and written in the same place as the recent ones.

briefdescription.para: The sequence should be finished immediately.
Action to be performed after a sampling is done.
Trigger fires whenever new data is ready.
Trigger fires when the FIFO watermark has been reached.
Trigger fires when the FIFO becomes full.
Number of all common adc triggers.
This and higher values are adc specific.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the adc header file.

briefdescription.para: Maximum value describing a adc trigger type.
ADC trigger types.
Include whatever data is associated with the trigger.
Do nothing with the associated trigger data, it may be consumed later.
Flush/clear whatever data is associated with the trigger.
Options for what to do with the associated data when a trigger is consumed.

memberdef.definition: typedef enum adc_action(* adc_sequence_callback) (const struct device *dev, const struct adc_sequence *sequence, uint16_t sampling_index)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, const struct adc_sequence *sequence, uint16_t sampling_index)
memberdef.name: adc_sequence_callback

briefdescription.para: Type definition of the optional callback function to be called after a requested sampling is done.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

Pointer to the sequence structure that triggered the sampling. This parameter points to a copy of the structure that was supplied to the call that started the sampling sequence, thus it cannot be used with the CONTAINER_OF() macro to retrieve some other data associated with the sequence. Instead, the adc_sequence_options::user_data field should be used for such purpose.

simplesect.para: Action to be performed by the driver. See adc_action.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* adc_api_channel_setup) (const struct device *dev, const struct adc_channel_cfg *channel_cfg)
memberdef.name: adc_api_channel_setup

briefdescription.para: Type definition of ADC API function for configuring a channel.

detaileddescription.para: See adc_channel_setup() for argument descriptions.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* adc_api_read) (const struct device *dev, const struct adc_sequence *sequence)
memberdef.name: adc_api_read

briefdescription.para: Type definition of ADC API function for setting a read request.

detaileddescription.para: See adc_read() for argument descriptions.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* adc_api_submit) (const struct device *dev, struct rtio_iodev_sqe *sqe)
memberdef.name: adc_api_submit

briefdescription.para: Type definition of ADC API function for submitting a stream request.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* adc_api_get_decoder) (const struct device *dev, const struct adc_decoder_api **api)
memberdef.name: adc_api_get_decoder

briefdescription.para: Get the decoder associate with the given device.

simplesect.para: adc_get_decoder() for more details

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* adc_api_read_async) (const struct device *dev, const struct adc_sequence *sequence, struct k_poll_signal *async)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, const struct adc_sequence *sequence, struct k_poll_signal *async)
memberdef.name: adc_api_read_async

briefdescription.para: Type definition of ADC API function for setting an asynchronous read request.

detaileddescription.para: See adc_read_async() for argument descriptions.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* adc_raw_to_x_fn) (int32_t ref_mv, enum adc_gain gain, uint8_t resolution, int32_t *valp)
memberdef.argsstring: )(int32_t ref_mv, enum adc_gain gain, uint8_t resolution, int32_t *valp)
memberdef.name: adc_raw_to_x_fn

briefdescription.para: Conversion from raw ADC units to a specific output unit.

detaileddescription.para: This function performs the necessary conversion to transform a raw ADC measurement to a physical voltage.

parameterdescription.para: the reference voltage used for the measurement, in millivolts. This may be from adc_ref_internal() or a known external reference.
the ADC gain configuration used to sample the input
the number of bits in the absolute value of the sample. For differential sampling this needs to be one less than the resolution in struct adc_sequence.
pointer to the raw measurement value on input, and the corresponding output value on successful conversion. If conversion fails the stored value is left unchanged.
if the gain is not reversible

briefdescription.para: Invert the application of gain to a measurement value.

detaileddescription.para: For example, if the gain passed in is ADC_GAIN_1_6 and the referenced value is 10, the value after the function returns is 60.

parameterdescription.para: the gain used to amplify the input signal.
a pointer to a value that initially has the effect of the applied gain but has that effect removed when this function successfully returns. If the gain cannot be reversed the value remains unchanged.
if the gain was successfully reversed
if the gain could not be interpreted

briefdescription.para: Invert the application of gain to a measurement value.

detaileddescription.para: For example, if the gain passed in is ADC_GAIN_1_6 and the referenced value is 10, the value after the function returns is 60.

parameterdescription.para: the gain used to amplify the input signal.
a pointer to a value that initially has the effect of the applied gain but has that effect removed when this function successfully returns. If the gain cannot be reversed the value remains unchanged.
if the gain was successfully reversed
if the gain could not be interpreted

briefdescription.para: Configure an ADC channel.

detaileddescription.para: It is required to call this function and configure each channel before it is selected for a read request.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
If a parameter with an invalid value has been provided.

briefdescription.para: Configure an ADC channel from a struct adc_dt_spec.

simplesect.para: A value from adc_channel_setup() or -ENOTSUP if information from Devicetree is not valid.

briefdescription.para: Set a read request.

detaileddescription.para:
dev
Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
sequence
Structure specifying requested sequence of samplings.
If invoked from user mode, any sequence struct options for callback must be NULL.

parameterdescription.para: If a parameter with an invalid value has been provided.
If the provided buffer is to small to hold the results of all requested samplings.
If the requested mode of operation is not supported.

If another sampling was triggered while the previous one was still in progress. This may occur only when samplings are done with intervals, and it indicates that the selected interval was too small. All requested samples are written in the buffer, but at least some of them were taken with an extra delay compared to what was scheduled.

briefdescription.para: Set a read request from a struct adc_dt_spec.

parameterdescription.para: Structure specifying requested sequence of samplings.

simplesect.para: A value from adc_read().

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct adc_sequence *sequence, struct k_poll_signal *async)
memberdef.name: adc_read_async

briefdescription.para: Set an asynchronous read request.

detaileddescription.para:
This function is available only if CONFIG_ADC_ASYNC is selected.
If invoked from user mode, any sequence struct options for callback must be NULL.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Structure specifying requested sequence of samplings.
Pointer to a valid and ready to be signaled struct k_poll_signal. (Note: if NULL this function will not notify the end of the transaction, and whether it went successfully or not).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative error code otherwise. See adc_read() for a list of possible error codes.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct adc_dt_spec *spec, const struct adc_sequence *sequence, struct k_poll_signal *async)
memberdef.name: adc_read_async_dt

briefdescription.para: Set an asynchronous read request from a struct adc_dt_spec.

detaileddescription.para:
This function is available only if CONFIG_ADC_ASYNC is selected.
If invoked from user mode, any sequence struct options for callback must be NULL.

parameterdescription.para: Structure specifying requested sequence of samplings.
Pointer to a valid and ready to be signaled struct k_poll_signal. (Note: if NULL this function will not notify the end of the transaction, and whether it went successfully or not).

simplesect.para: A value from adc_read().

briefdescription.para: Get decoder APIs for that device.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_ADC_STREAM.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to the decoder which will be set upon success.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative error code otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio_iodev *iodev, struct rtio *ctx, void *userdata, struct rtio_sqe **handle)
memberdef.name: adc_stream

briefdescription.para: Start a continuous ADC streaming session.

detaileddescription.para: Submits a multishot read request to the RTIO context associated with the given IO device.
The ADC driver will continuously produce completion queue entries (CQEs) as samples become available. These CQEs can be consumed using rtio_cqe_consume_block().

Since the sample data itself is stored in a memory pool, the application must then get the buffer using rtio_cqe_get_mempool_buffer() before decoding the data with the driver's adc_decoder_api. After processing, the buffer and CQE must be released.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_ADC_STREAM.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the RTIO IO device created with ADC_DT_STREAM_IODEV.
Pointer to the RTIO context (with memory pool) that will receive the streaming data.
Optional user data pointer passed through to CQEs.
Optional output pointer; if non-NULL, receives the submission queue entry that can be used to cancel the stream.
An SQE could not be acquired from the RTIO context.

briefdescription.para: Get the internal reference voltage.

detaileddescription.para: Returns the voltage corresponding to ADC_REF_INTERNAL, measured in millivolts.

simplesect.para: a positive value is the reference voltage value. Returns zero if reference voltage information is not available.

memberdef.argsstring: (int32_t ref_mv, enum adc_gain gain, uint8_t resolution, int32_t *valp)
memberdef.name: adc_raw_to_millivolts

briefdescription.para: Convert a raw ADC value to millivolts.

memberdef.argsstring: (int32_t ref_mv, enum adc_gain gain, uint8_t resolution, int32_t *valp)
memberdef.name: adc_raw_to_microvolts

briefdescription.para: Convert a raw ADC value to microvolts.

memberdef.argsstring: (adc_raw_to_x_fn conv_func, const struct adc_dt_spec *spec, const struct adc_channel_cfg *channel_cfg, int32_t *valp)
memberdef.name: adc_raw_to_x_dt_chan

briefdescription.para: Convert a raw ADC value to an arbitrary output unit.

parameterdescription.para: Function that converts to the final output unit.
Channel configuration used for sampling. This can be either the configuration from spec, or an alternate sampling configuration based on spec, for example a different gain value.
Pointer to the raw measurement value on input, and the corresponding output value on successful conversion. If conversion fails the stored value is left unchanged.

simplesect.para: A value from adc_raw_to_x_fn or -ENOTSUP if information from Devicetree is not valid.

briefdescription.para: Convert a raw ADC value to millivolts using information stored in a struct adc_dt_spec.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the raw measurement value on input, and the corresponding millivolt value on successful conversion. If conversion fails the stored value is left unchanged.

simplesect.para: A value from adc_raw_to_millivolts() or -ENOTSUP if information from Devicetree is not valid.

briefdescription.para: Convert a raw ADC value to microvolts using information stored in a struct adc_dt_spec.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the raw measurement value on input, and the corresponding microvolt value on successful conversion. If conversion fails the stored value is left unchanged.

simplesect.para: A value from adc_raw_to_microvolts() or -ENOTSUP if information from Devicetree is not valid.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a struct adc_sequence from information stored in struct adc_dt_spec.

detaileddescription.para: Note that this function only initializes the following fields:
Other fields should be initialized by the caller.

parameterdescription.para: If spec does not have valid channel configuration

briefdescription.para: Validate that the ADC device is ready.

simplesect.para: true if the ADC device is ready for use and false otherwise.

memberdef.name: ADC_CHANNEL_CFG_DT
memberdef.initializer: { \ .gain = DT_STRING_TOKEN(node_id, zephyr_gain), \ .reference = DT_STRING_TOKEN(node_id, zephyr_reference), \ .acquisition_time = DT_PROP(node_id, zephyr_acquisition_time), \ .channel_id = DT_REG_ADDR(node_id), \ COND_CODE_1(UTIL_OR(DT_PROP(node_id, zephyr_differential), \ UTIL_AND(CONFIG_ADC_CONFIGURABLE_INPUTS, \ DT_NODE_HAS_PROP(node_id, zephyr_input_negative))), \ (.differential = true,), \ (.differential = false,)) \ IF_ENABLED(CONFIG_ADC_CONFIGURABLE_INPUTS, \ (.input_positive = DT_PROP_OR(node_id, zephyr_input_positive, 0), \ .input_negative = DT_PROP_OR(node_id, zephyr_input_negative, 0),)) \ IF_ENABLED(CONFIG_ADC_CONFIGURABLE_EXCITATION_CURRENT_SOURCE_PIN, \ (.current_source_pin_set = DT_NODE_HAS_PROP(node_id, zephyr_current_source_pin), \ .current_source_pin = DT_PROP_OR(node_id, zephyr_current_source_pin, {0}),)) \ IF_ENABLED(CONFIG_ADC_CONFIGURABLE_VBIAS_PIN, \ (.vbias_pins = DT_PROP_OR(node_id, zephyr_vbias_pins, 0),)) \ }

briefdescription.para: Get ADC channel configuration from a given devicetree node.

detaileddescription.para: This returns a static initializer for a struct adc_channel_cfg filled with data from a given devicetree node.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example usage:

simplesect.para: Static initializer for an adc_channel_cfg structure.

briefdescription.para: Get ADC io-channel information from devicetree by name.

detaileddescription.para: This returns a static initializer for an adc_dt_spec structure given a devicetree node and a channel name. The node must have the "io-channels" property defined.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example usage:

simplesect.para: Static initializer for an adc_dt_spec structure.

briefdescription.para: Like ADC_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_NAME(), with a fallback to a default value.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct adc_dt_spec for the property, or default_value if the node or property do not exist.

briefdescription.para: Get ADC io-channel information from a DT_DRV_COMPAT devicetree instance by name.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for an adc_dt_spec structure.

briefdescription.para: Like ADC_DT_SPEC_INST_GET_BY_NAME(), with a fallback to a default value.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct adc_dt_spec for the property, or default_value if the node or property do not exist.

briefdescription.para: Get ADC io-channel information from devicetree.

detaileddescription.para: This returns a static initializer for an adc_dt_spec structure given a devicetree node and a channel index. The node must have the "io-channels" property defined.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example usage:

simplesect.para: Static initializer for an adc_dt_spec structure.

briefdescription.para: Like ADC_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_IDX(), with a fallback to a default value.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct adc_dt_spec for the property, or default_value if the node or property do not exist.

briefdescription.para: Get ADC io-channel information from a DT_DRV_COMPAT devicetree instance.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for an adc_dt_spec structure.

briefdescription.para: Like ADC_DT_SPEC_INST_GET_BY_IDX(), with a fallback to a default value.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct adc_dt_spec for the property, or default_value if the node or property do not exist.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to ADC_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_IDX(node_id, 0).

simplesect.para: Static initializer for an adc_dt_spec structure.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to ADC_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_IDX_OR(node_id, 0, default_value).

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct adc_dt_spec for the property, or default_value if the node or property do not exist.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to ADC_DT_SPEC_INST_GET_BY_IDX(inst, 0).

simplesect.para: Static initializer for an adc_dt_spec structure.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to ADC_DT_SPEC_INST_GET_BY_IDX_OR(inst, 0, default).

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct adc_dt_spec for the property, or default_value if the node or property do not exist.

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for Analog-to-Digital Converters (ADC).
